predictions.notes: High probability of debt default event in 2025 without restructuring

anomalies.description: SGR freight revenue 35% below forecast for Q2 FY2024; significant deviation from projected ramp-up curve
anomalies.investigation: Revenue shortfall attributed to competition from road transport; freight incentive programme being developed

anomalies.description: Unusual spike in transformer replacement costs in Q1 FY2024, 180% above historical average
anomalies.investigation: Explained by accelerated grid upgrade programme following August 2023 blackout

anomalies.description: Single-source procurement of aircraft spare parts without competitive tendering, value KES 850 million
anomalies.investigation: KQ claims emergency procurement justified by AOG (Aircraft on Ground) situation; documentation being reviewed

anomalies.description: Port dues collection 12% above forecast in September 2024, driven by unexpected surge in bulk cargo vessels
anomalies.investigation: Positive variance explained by diversion of vessels from Dar es Salaam due to port congestion

insights.title: Regional Power Export Revenue Opportunity
insights.description: Analysis of Eastern Africa Power Pool data shows Uganda and Tanzania willing to pay premium rates for reliable geothermal baseload power. KenGen could earn additional KES 4.5 billion annually from exports.
insights.recommendedAction: Accelerate EAPP interconnection negotiations; develop export pricing strategy; engage KETRACO on cross-border transmission capacity

insights.title: SGR Default Risk — Immediate Action Required
insights.description: Debt service coverage ratio has fallen to 0.24x (target: 1.5x). Without restructuring, KRC will default on Exim Bank obligations in Q1 2025, triggering sovereign guarantee activation.
insights.recommendedAction: Immediate engagement with National Treasury and Exim Bank; appoint debt restructuring advisor; develop 5-year financial recovery plan

insights.title: Smart Meter ROI Acceleration
insights.description: Analysis shows smart meter rollout is reducing non-technical losses by 8% in completed zones. Accelerating rollout to all Nairobi zones could recover KES 12 billion in annual revenue leakage.
insights.recommendedAction: Increase smart meter installation rate from 50,000 to 100,000 per month; prioritise high-loss zones in Nairobi and Mombasa

insights.title: Board Gender Diversity Below Mwongozo Target
insights.description: Portfolio-wide analysis shows average female board representation of 32%, below the Mwongozo Code target of 40%. KRC (18%) and KPC (22%) are significantly below target.
insights.recommendedAction: National Treasury to issue directive on board diversity; prioritise female candidates in next board appointment cycle for KRC and KPC

benchmarks.metricName: Cost per Available Seat Kilometre (CASK) — KES cents
